puzzle.title: The Warning Carried on the Wind
puzzle.intro: A messenger was sent to a people of immense power and arrogance, who built magnificent structures as if they would live forever. This individual's call to truth was met with scorn and ridicule, but their warning of a coming storm was a test of faith that would shake the very foundations of their civilization. Can you identify this steadfast soul?
clues: I was sent to my own brethren, a people known for their great physical strength and architectural skill.
I warned them about their pride in their constructions, asking if they built landmarks on every high place for mere amusement.
My people accused me of foolishness and falsehood, rejecting my call to fear Allah and obey my message.
They challenged me to bring the punishment I spoke of, confident in the power of their idols and their own might.
I reminded them of how Allah had made them successors after the people of Nuh, and had increased them in stature.
When they saw a dense cloud approaching, they rejoiced thinking it was rain, but I knew it was the destructive, furious wind that Allah had promised.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Prophet Salih
Prophet Hud
Prophet Nuh
Prophet Shu'ayb
Prophet Lut

trial.label: What Was My Primary Trial?
options: Convincing my people to abandon a golden calf.
Calling the arrogant people of 'Ad to monotheism.
Dealing with the deceit of my brothers in a well.
Confronting a king who claimed divinity and threw me into a fire.
Persuading my people to stop cheating in weights and measures.
trial.answer: Calling the arrogant people of 'Ad to monotheism.

location.label: What Was the Punishment?
options: A great flood that covered the earth.
A shower of stones of baked clay.
A furious and destructive wind (Sarsar).
A mighty earthquake and a terrible cry (Sayhah).
Being transformed into apes.

outcome.label: What Was the Outcome?
options: I was swallowed by a giant fish but later saved.
I and the believers with me were saved by Allah's mercy.
I was raised to the heavens without experiencing death.
I was given a kingdom and the ability to understand animals.
My people all believed and their punishment was averted.
outcome.answer: I and the believers with me were saved by Allah's mercy.

puzzle.arabic: ۞ وَٱذْكُرْ أَخَا عَادٍ إِذْ أَنذَرَ قَوْمَهُۥ بِٱلْأَحْقَافِ وَقَدْ خَلَتِ ٱلنُّذُرُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِۦٓ أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوٓا۟ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍۢ
puzzle.verse: And mention, [O Muḥammad], the brother of ʿAad, when he warned his people in [the region of] al-Aḥqāf - and warners had already passed on before him and after him - [saying], "Do not worship except Allāh. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a terrible day." (46:21)
